docs.title: Home
docs.text:
What is Stylebook?

  • Library extension A set of additional rules for static code analysis tools
  • Simplified CLI One command to lint multiple file types

How does it work?

flowchart LR
  subgraph "Project"
    src(Source code)
    config1[Optional config files]
  end
  subgraph "Static analysis"
    linters[Linter libraries]
    extensions[Rule extensions]
    config2[Default config files]
  end
  subgraph "Report"
    cli[Build failure<br>on lint errors]
  end
  subgraph "Refactor"
    manual(Manual edits<br>using suggestions)
  end

  src -- run --> linters
  extensions -- CLI --> cli
  cli -- fix --> manual

docs.title: Support list
docs.text:
Many thanks to the communities who maintain the linter tools!

One CLI to rule them all

  • Blinter

    Blinter is a linter for Windows batch files.

    PyPI

  • checkmake

    Linter/analyzer for Makefiles.

  • ChkTeX

    LaTeX semantic checker.

    Website

  • Stylelint

    A mighty CSS linter that helps you avoid errors and enforce conventions.

    NPM Website

  • csvlint

    Library and command line tool that validates a CSV file.

  • dotenv-linter

    ☺️ Linting dotenv files like a charm!

    PyPI Website

  • gomoddirectives

    A linter that handle directives into go.mod.

  • hadolint

    Dockerfile linter, validate inline bash, written in Haskell.

  • HTMLHint

    ⚙️ The static code analysis tool you need for your HTML.

    NPM Website

  • JSON Lint

    JSON/CJSON/JSON5 parser, syntax & schema validator and pretty-printer with a command-line client, written in pure JavaScript.

    NPM Website

  • lockfile-lint

    Lint an npm or yarn lockfile to analyze and detect security issues.

    NPM

  • libxml2

    A Pluggable Terraform Linter.

  • Maid

    A lightweight Mermaid diagram linter which helps humans and AI with perfect diagrams.

    NPM Website

  • markdownlint

    A Node.js style checker and lint tool for Markdown/CommonMark files.

    NPM

  • propertieslint

    A linter for .properties file.

  • protolint

    A pluggable linter and fixer to enforce Protocol Buffer style and conventions.

  • py-taplo

    Python wrapper for taplo TOML toolkit.

    PyPI Website

  • pyinilint

    A linter for INI-like configuration files which are intended to be parsed by the standard Python configparser module.

    PyPI

  • ShellCheck

    ShellCheck, a static analysis tool for shell scripts.

    Website

  • SQLFluff

    A modular SQL linter and auto-formatter with support for multiple dialects and templated code.

    PyPI Website

  • TFLint

    A Pluggable Terraform Linter.

  • yamllint

    A linter for YAML files.

    PyPI

docs.title: Download
docs.text:
Get the artifacts from official package managers.

Download the library

PyPI NPM

docs.title: Decimal values
docs.text:
Omit leading and trailing zeros in decimal values.

Before

foo: 0.5
bar: 1.0

After

foo: .5
bar: 1

Tip

This rule is inverted on JSON5.

docs.title: Double quotes
docs.text:
Double quotes for strings. Quotes are optional in YAML, but must not be single quotes, unless the string itself contains double quotes.

Before

foo: 'foo'
bar: "\"bar\""

After

foo: "foo"
bar: '"bar"'

Warning

Expressions in GitHub Actions must be wrapped in single quotes.

docs.title: Duplicate blank line
docs.text:
No more than one consecutive blank line.

Before

Foo


Bar

After

Foo

Bar

Tip

In JSON, no blank lines are allowed at all.

docs.title: Indent style
docs.text:
Spaces over hard tabs for indentation, default 2 spaces.

Before

- Foo
    - Bar
        - Baz

After

- Foo
  - Bar
    - Baz

docs.title: Line length
docs.text:
The column limit is 80 characters.

Before

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Donec a diam lectus. Sed sit amet ipsum.

After

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Donec a diam lectus.
Sed sit amet ipsum.

Tip

There are some exceptions:

  • URLs are allowed to exceed the limit in most formats.
  • In Markdown, headings, tables and inline HTML are also fine.
  • Ignored in JSON.

docs.title: Trailing comma
docs.text:
In languages that support trailing commas, they should be used in multi-line structures.

Before

[parent]
child = [
    "foo",
    "bar"
]

After

[parent]
child = [
    "foo",
    "bar",
]

docs.title: Trailing newline
docs.text:
Files should end with a single newline character.

Before

Foo

After

Foo
↵

docs.title: Trailing space
docs.text:
No trailing spaces at the end of lines.

Before

Foo␣

After

Foo

docs.title: Unnecessary leading blank line
docs.text:
Files should not start with blank lines.

Before

Foo

After

Foo

docs.title: Lowercase syntax
docs.text:
Use lowercase for all CSS syntax.

Before

BODY {
  BACKGROUND-COLOR: RED;
}

After

body {
  background-color: red;
}

docs.title: Indent style
docs.text:
Use two spaces for indentation.

Before

body {
    background-color: red;
}

After

body {
  background-color: red;
}

docs.title: Command join
docs.text:
Commands in a Dockerfile should be joined together to reduce the number of layers in the resulting image.

Before

RUN apt-get update
RUN apt-get install -y curl

After

RUN apt-get update && \
  apt-get install -y curl

docs.title: Missing yes flag
docs.text:
Installing a package in a Dockerfile needs a confirmation flag to avoid interactive prompts.

Before

RUN apt-get install curl

After

RUN apt-get install -y curl

docs.title: Duplicate ID
docs.text:
IDs should be unique.

Before

<div id="foo">Foo</div>
<div id="foo">Bar</div>

After

<div id="foo">Foo</div>
<div id="bar">Bar</div>

docs.title: Missing component
docs.text:
Many tags require specific attributes.

Before

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
  <head>
    <title>Foo</title>
  </head>
  <body>
    <button>Click me</button>
    <input type="text"/>
    <img src=""/>
  </body>
</html>

After

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="description" content="Foo">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Foo</title>
  </head>
  <body>
    <button type="button">Click me</button>
    <input type="text" aria-label="Input"/>
    <img src="foo.png" alt="Foo image"/>
  </body>
</html>

docs.title: Lowercase syntax
docs.text:
Tags and attributes should be lowercase.

Before

<DIV CLASS="foo">Foo</DIV>

After

<div class="foo">Foo</div>

docs.title: JSON rules
docs.text: jsonlint doesn't have built-in rules.

docs.title: Blank line
docs.text:
No blank lines allowed.

Before

{
  "foo": "bar",

  "baz": "qux"
}

After

{
  "foo": "bar",
  "baz": "qux"
}

docs.title: Trailing comma
docs.text:
No trailing commas in JSON5 files.

Before

{
  "foo": "bar",
}

After

{
  "foo": "bar"
}

docs.title: Comment
docs.text:
No comments allowed in JSONC files.

Before

{
  // This is a comment.
  "foo": "bar"
}

After

{
  "foo": "bar"
}

docs.title: Ordered list number
docs.text:
Use lazy numbering in ordered lists. The prefix may start with any number, but the rest should follow the first one.

Before

1.  Foo
2.  Bar
3.  Baz

---

4.  Foo2
5.  Bar2
6.  Baz2

After

1.  Foo
1.  Bar
1.  Baz

---

4.  Foo2
4.  Bar2
4.  Baz2

docs.title: Broken image
docs.text:
Image URLs should return a correct content type.

Before

![Favicon](https://google.com/)

After

![Favicon](https://www.google.com/favicon.ico)
Configuration Setting Default value timeout 6,000 ignored_hosts npmjs.com, www.npmjs.com

docs.title: Dead link
docs.text:
Link URLs should return a non-error status code.

Before

[Google](https://googlea.com/)

After

[Google](https://www.google.com/)
Configuration Setting Default value timeout 6,000 ignored_hosts npmjs.com, www.npmjs.com

docs.title: Duplicate link
docs.text:
Inline links should be unique. Use reference links for duplicates.

Before

Visit [Google](https://www.google.com).

Don't forget to visit [Google](https://www.google.com).

After

Visit [Google][google].

Don't forget to visit [Google][google].

[google]: https://www.google.com

docs.title: Code fence style
docs.text:
Backticks style over tilde in code fences.

Before

~~~js
console.log("Hello, world!");
~~~

After

```js
console.log("Hello, world!");
```

docs.title: Heading style
docs.text:
ATX heading style over Setext.

Before

Big headline
============

After

# Big headline

docs.title: Highlight style
docs.text:
Emphasis and styles use asterisks first. In nested hightlight, use any non-asterisk character as the outer style.

Before

Hello **_World_**

After

Hello __*World*__

docs.title: Horizontal line style
docs.text:
Triple dash style over asterisk and underscore in horizontal lines.

Before

Foo

***

Bar

After

Foo

---

Bar

docs.title: Table style
docs.text:
Pipes are present with short column style. Exactly one space padding on each side of cell.

Before

Foo | Bar  | Baz
--- | ---- | ----
Qux | Quux | Quuz

After

| Foo | Bar | Baz |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Qux | Quux | Quuz |

docs.title: Unordered list style
docs.text:
Dash style over asterisk and plus in unordered lists.

Before

* Foo
+ Bar

After

- Foo
- Bar

docs.title: Ordered list spaces
docs.text:
The length of ordered list number and the following space should be even number.

Before

1. Foo
1. Bar
1. Baz

---

10.  Foo2
10.  Bar2
10.  Baz2

After

1.  Foo
1.  Bar
1.  Baz

---

10. Foo2
10. Bar2
10. Baz2

docs.title: Unnecessary blank lines in list
docs.text:
If no list item is expanded with a paragraph, remove blank lines between them. A blank line between one list item and a nested list is not checked.

Before

- Foo

- Bar

- Baz

After

- Foo
- Bar
- Baz

docs.title: Lowercase identifiers
docs.text:
SQL identifiers should be lowercase.

Before

CREATE TABLE Users (
  Id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  Name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
);

After

CREATE TABLE users (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
);

docs.title: Lowercase functions
docs.text:
SQL functions should be lowercase.

Before

SELECT COUNT(*)
  FROM users
  WHERE active = TRUE;

After

SELECT count(*)
  FROM users
  WHERE active = TRUE;

docs.title: Uppercase keywords
docs.text:
SQL keywords should be uppercase.

Before

select *
  from users
  where id = 1;

After

SELECT *
  FROM users
  WHERE id = 1;

docs.title: Uppercase literals
docs.text:
SQL literals should be uppercase.

Before

SELECT *
  FROM users
  WHERE active = true;

After

SELECT *
  FROM users
  WHERE active = TRUE;

docs.title: Uppercase types
docs.text:
SQL types should be uppercase.

Before

CREATE TABLE users (
  id int PRIMARY KEY,
  name varchar(255) NOT NULL
);

After

CREATE TABLE users (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY,
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
);

docs.title: Horizontal alignment
docs.text:
Entries and comments are not aligned horizontally.

Before

[parent]
foo  = "bar"   # comment
foo2 = "bar2"  # comment2

After

[parent]
foo = "bar"  # comment
foo2 = "bar2"  # comment2

docs.title: Boolean values
docs.text:
Only true and false are allowed as boolean values.

Before

foo: on
bar: OFF

After

foo: true
bar: false
